# bd - Beads

Beads (bd) is a distributed, git-backed graph issue tracker designed specifically for AI agents. It provides persistent, structured memory for coding agents, replacing messy markdown plans with a dependency-aware graph that allows agents to handle long-horizon tasks without losing context. The system is powered by Dolt, a version-controlled SQL database with cell-level merge, native branching, and built-in sync via Dolt remotes.

The core architecture uses hash-based IDs (`bd-a1b2`) to prevent merge collisions in multi-agent/multi-branch workflows. It supports hierarchical epics with dotted IDs (e.g., `bd-a3f8.1`, `bd-a3f8.1.1`), dependency tracking between issues, and a "molecular chemistry" metaphor for template-based workflows. Beads integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, and Copilot, providing JSON output for programmatic use and atomic claim operations for preventing race conditions when multiple agents work concurrently.

## Initialize Repository

Initialize beads in a project to create the `.beads/` directory and Dolt database. The wizard prompts for role configuration and optionally installs git hooks.

```bash
# Basic setup (prompts for contributor mode)
bd init

# OSS contributor (fork workflow with separate planning repo)
bd init --contributor

# Team member (branch workflow for collaboration)
bd init --team

# Protected main branch (GitHub/GitLab) - uses dedicated sync branch
bd init --branch beads-sync

# Stealth mode (local only, not committed to main repo)
bd init --stealth

# Check database status
bd info --json
# Output:
# {
#   "database_path": "/path/to/.beads/beads.db",
#   "issue_prefix": "bd",
#   "agent_mail_enabled": false
# }
```

## Create Issues

Create issues with priorities, types, labels, and hierarchical relationships. Supports reading descriptions from files or stdin to avoid shell escaping issues.

```bash
# Basic creation with type and priority
bd create "Fix authentication bug" -t bug -p 1 -d "Token validation fails" --json
# Output: {"id": "bd-a3f8", "title": "Fix authentication bug", ...}

# Create with labels
bd create "Critical security fix" -t bug -p 0 -l security,urgent --json

# Create epic with hierarchical children
bd create "Auth System" -t epic -p 1 --json                     # Returns: bd-a3f8e9
bd create "Login UI" -p 1 --parent bd-a3f8e9 --json             # Auto-assigned: bd-a3f8e9.1
bd create "Backend validation" -p 1 --parent bd-a3f8e9 --json   # Auto-assigned: bd-a3f8e9.2
bd create "Tests" -p 1 --parent bd-a3f8e9 --json                # Auto-assigned: bd-a3f8e9.3

# Create with description from file (avoids shell escaping issues)
bd create "Complex feature" --body-file=description.md -p 1 --json

# Read description from stdin
echo "Description with backticks and special chars" | bd create "Title" --stdin --json

# Create and link discovered work in one command
bd create "Found bug during testing" -t bug -p 1 --deps discovered-from:bd-parent --json

# Create with external reference to GitHub/Jira
bd create "Fix login" -t bug -p 1 --external-ref "gh-123" --json
```

## Find Ready Work

Find issues with no open blocking dependencies. The ready queue shows only work that can be started immediately.

```bash
# List all ready work (no blockers, not claimed)
bd ready --json
# Output:
# [
#   {"id": "bd-a1b2", "title": "Set up database", "priority": 1, "type": "task"},
#   {"id": "bd-c3d4", "title": "Create API", "priority": 2, "type": "feature"}
# ]

# Filter ready work by criteria
bd ready --priority 1 --json              # High priority only
bd ready --label backend --json           # By label
bd ready --assignee alice --json          # By assignee
bd ready --unassigned --json              # Unassigned only
bd ready --type task --json               # By issue type
bd ready --sort oldest --json             # Oldest first

# Find stale issues (not updated recently)
bd stale --days 30 --json                 # Default: 30 days
bd stale --days 90 --status in_progress --json  # Find abandoned claims
```

## Claim and Update Issues

Atomically claim issues to prevent race conditions when multiple agents work concurrently. The claim operation sets assignee and status in one atomic transaction.

```bash
# Atomically claim an issue (fails if already claimed)
bd update bd-a1b2 --claim --json
# Output: {"id": "bd-a1b2", "status": "in_progress", "assignee": "agent-1"}

# Update multiple issues at once
bd update bd-41 bd-42 bd-43 --priority 0 --json

# Update specific fields
bd update bd-a1b2 --status in_progress --json
bd update bd-a1b2 --priority 1 --json
bd update bd-a1b2 --assignee alice --json
bd update bd-a1b2 --description "Updated description" --json

# Update external reference
bd update bd-a1b2 --external-ref "jira-PROJ-789" --json
```

## Close and Reopen Issues

Mark work as completed or reopen closed issues. Supports batch operations on multiple issues.

```bash
# Close a single issue
bd close bd-a1b2 --reason "Implemented and tested" --json
# Output: {"id": "bd-a1b2", "status": "closed"}

# Close multiple issues at once
bd close bd-41 bd-42 bd-43 --reason "Batch completion" --json

# Reopen closed issues
bd reopen bd-a1b2 --reason "Found additional bugs" --json
```

## View Issues

Get detailed information about issues including dependencies, audit trail, and related links.

```bash
# Show single issue details
bd show bd-a1b2 --json
# Output:
# {
#   "id": "bd-a1b2",
#   "title": "Fix authentication",
#   "status": "open",
#   "priority": 1,
#   "type": "bug",
#   "dependencies": ["bd-c3d4"],
#   "labels": ["security", "urgent"]
# }

# Show multiple issues
bd show bd-a1b2 bd-c3d4 --json

# Show currently active issue (in-progress or last touched)
bd show --current --json
```

## List and Filter Issues

Query issues with powerful filtering options including status, priority, labels, text search, and date ranges.

```bash
# Basic filters
bd list --status open --priority 1 --json              # Status and priority
bd list --assignee alice --json                        # By assignee
bd list --type bug --json                              # By issue type
bd list --id bd-123,bd-456 --json                      # Specific IDs

# Label filters
bd list --label bug,critical --json                    # AND: must have ALL
bd list --label-any frontend,backend --json            # OR: has ANY

# Text search
bd list --title "auth" --json                          # Title substring
bd list --title-contains "auth" --json                 # Case-insensitive title
bd list --desc-contains "implement" --json             # Description search
bd list --notes-contains "TODO" --json                 # Notes search

# Date range filters
bd list --created-after 2024-01-01 --json
bd list --updated-before 2024-12-31 --json
bd list --closed-after 2024-01-01 --json

# Empty/null checks
bd list --empty-description --json                     # No description
bd list --no-assignee --json                           # Unassigned
bd list --no-labels --json                             # No labels

# Priority ranges
bd list --priority-min 0 --priority-max 1 --json       # P0 and P1 only

# Combine filters
bd list --status open --priority 1 --label-any urgent,critical --no-assignee --json
```

## Manage Dependencies

Create dependencies between issues to control execution order. Only `blocks` dependencies affect the ready work queue.

```bash
# Add blocking dependency (issue-2 depends on issue-1)
bd dep add bd-issue-2 bd-issue-1
# Now bd-issue-2 won't appear in bd ready until bd-issue-1 is closed

# Shorthand: issue-1 blocks issue-2
bd dep bd-issue-1 --blocks bd-issue-2

# Remove dependency
bd dep remove bd-issue-2 bd-issue-1

# Specify dependency type
bd dep add bd-issue-2 bd-issue-1 --type tracks          # Non-blocking
bd dep add bd-issue-2 bd-issue-1 --type caused-by       # Root cause link
bd dep add bd-issue-2 bd-issue-1 --type discovered-from # Found during work
bd dep add bd-issue-2 bd-issue-1 --type conditional-blocks # Runs if A fails

# View dependency tree
bd dep tree bd-a1b2
# Output:
# Dependency tree for bd-a1b2:
# → bd-a1b2: Add authentication [P2] (open)
#   → bd-c3d4: Create API [P2] (open)
#     → bd-e5f6: Set up database [P1] (open)

# View reverse dependencies (what depends on this)
bd dep tree bd-a1b2 --direction=up

# List dependencies
bd dep list bd-a1b2 --direction=up                     # What depends on this?
bd dep list bd-a1b2 --type=tracks                      # Filter by type

# Detect cycles
bd dep cycles
```

## Manage Labels

Add, remove, and query labels on issues. Supports batch operations on multiple issues.

```bash
# Add label to issues
bd label add bd-a1b2 security --json
bd label add bd-41 bd-42 bd-43 urgent --json           # Multiple issues

# Remove label
bd label remove bd-a1b2 security --json

# List labels on an issue
bd label list bd-a1b2 --json
# Output: {"labels": ["security", "backend"]}

# List all labels in database
bd label list-all --json
```

## Operational State Management

Track operational state on role beads using the `<dimension>:<value>` label convention. State transitions are recorded as events for audit trail.

```bash
# Query current state value
bd state bd-witness patrol
# Output: active

# Query with JSON output
bd state --json bd-witness patrol
# Output: {"issue_id": "bd-witness", "dimension": "patrol", "value": "active"}

# List all state dimensions on an issue
bd state list bd-witness --json
# Output: {"patrol": "active", "mode": "normal", "health": "healthy"}

# Set state (creates event + updates label atomically)
bd set-state bd-witness patrol=muted --reason "Investigating stuck polecat"
bd set-state bd-witness mode=degraded --reason "High error rate"
bd set-state bd-witness health=warning --reason "Memory usage high"
```

## Gates (External Condition Blocking)

Gates block dependent work until external conditions are met (PR merges, CI passes, timers).

```bash
# Create PR merge gate
bd create --type=gate --title="Wait for PR #42" \
  --await-type=gh:pr --await-id=42

# Create CI run gate
bd create --type=gate --title="Wait for CI" \
  --await-type=gh:run --await-id=12345

# Create timer gate (wait 30 minutes)
bd create --type=gate --title="Cooldown" \
  --await-type=timer --await-id=30m

# Create cross-rig bead gate
bd create --type=gate --title="Wait for upstream fix" \
  --await-type=bead --await-id=other-rig:issue-id

# Wire gate into dependencies
bd dep add bd-issue-2 bd-gate-id

# Check and auto-close resolved gates
bd gate check                            # All gates
bd gate check --type=gh:pr               # Only PR gates
bd gate check --dry-run                  # Preview without changes
bd gate check --escalate                 # Escalate failed gates

# List and inspect gates
bd gate list                             # Open gates
bd gate list --all                       # Including closed
bd gate show bd-gate-id                  # Full details

# Manually resolve gate
bd gate resolve bd-gate-id --reason "Approved by team lead"

# Auto-discover CI run IDs
bd gate discover --dry-run --branch main
```

## Molecular Chemistry (Template Workflows)

Beads uses a chemistry metaphor for template-based workflows: protos (templates), mols (persistent instances), and wisps (ephemeral instances).

```bash
# List available formulas (templates)
bd formula list --json

# Show proto structure and variables
bd mol show proto-auth --json

# Pour: instantiate proto as persistent mol (solid → liquid)
bd mol pour proto-auth --var project=myapp --json
bd mol pour proto-auth --var project=myapp --dry-run  # Preview

# Wisp: instantiate proto as ephemeral wisp (solid → vapor)
bd mol wisp proto-patrol --var target=service-a --json

# List wisps
bd mol wisp list --json
bd mol wisp list --all --json            # Include closed

# Bond: connect work graphs
bd mol bond mol-A mol-B --json                         # Sequential (default)
bd mol bond mol-A mol-B --type parallel --json         # Parallel
bd mol bond mol-A mol-B --type conditional --json      # B runs if A fails

# Squash: compress wisp to permanent digest
bd mol squash bd-wisp-id --summary "Work completed" --json

# Burn: delete wisp without digest
bd mol burn bd-wisp-id --json
bd mol burn bd-wisp-id --force --json    # Skip confirmation

# Garbage collect orphaned wisps
bd mol wisp gc --json
bd mol wisp gc --age 24h --json          # Custom age threshold
bd mol wisp gc --closed --force          # Purge all closed wisps
```

## Sync and Version Control

Synchronize beads data with git and Dolt remotes. Dolt provides cell-level merge for conflict resolution.

```bash
# Manual sync (commit/push)
bd sync

# Import issues from JSONL
bd import -i .beads/issues.jsonl --dry-run      # Preview
bd import -i .beads/issues.jsonl                # Import
bd import -i .beads/issues.jsonl --dedupe-after # Import + detect duplicates

# Handle missing parents during import
bd import -i issues.jsonl --orphan-handling allow      # Default
bd import -i issues.jsonl --orphan-handling resurrect  # Auto-resurrect
bd import -i issues.jsonl --orphan-handling skip       # Skip with warning
bd import -i issues.jsonl --orphan-handling strict     # Fail if missing

# Migrate databases after version upgrade
bd migrate --dry-run                                   # Preview
bd migrate                                             # Apply migration
bd migrate --cleanup --yes                             # Migrate and remove old files

# Setup sync branch for protected branches
bd migrate sync beads-sync                             # Create orphan sync branch
bd migrate sync beads-sync --dry-run                   # Preview
```

## Configuration

Configure tool-level settings (Viper) and project-level settings (bd config).

```bash
# Set project configuration
bd config set jira.url "https://company.atlassian.net"
bd config set jira.project "PROJ"
bd config set issue_id_mode counter                    # Sequential IDs

# Get configuration
bd config get jira.url
bd config get --json jira.url
# Output: {"key": "jira.url", "value": "https://company.atlassian.net"}

# List all configuration
bd config list --json

# Unset configuration
bd config unset jira.url

# Common settings
bd config set max_collision_prob "0.25"                # Hash ID collision tolerance
bd config set min_hash_length "4"                      # Minimum hash length
bd config set import.orphan_handling "resurrect"       # Orphan handling mode
bd config set create.require-description "true"        # Require descriptions
```

## Database Administration

Manage database cleanup, compaction, and maintenance operations.

```bash
# Clean up closed issues
bd admin cleanup --dry-run --json                      # Preview
bd admin cleanup --force --json                        # Delete ALL closed
bd admin cleanup --older-than 30 --force --json        # Delete >30 days old
bd admin cleanup --older-than 90 --cascade --force --json  # Delete + dependents

# Compaction (memory decay)
bd admin compact --stats --json                        # Show statistics
bd admin compact --analyze --json                      # Get candidates
bd admin compact --analyze --tier 1 --limit 10 --json  # Limited batch
bd admin compact --apply --id bd-42 --summary summary.txt  # Apply compaction

# Restore compacted issue from git history
bd restore bd-42

# Find orphaned issues (referenced in git but not closed)
bd orphans --json
bd orphans --db ~/other-repo/.beads/beads.db           # Cross-repo

# Find and merge duplicate issues
bd duplicates                                          # Show all
bd duplicates --auto-merge                             # Auto merge
bd merge bd-42 bd-43 --into bd-41 --json               # Manual merge

# Reset (remove all local beads data)
bd admin reset                                         # Preview
bd admin reset --force                                 # Apply reset

# Rename issue prefix
bd rename-prefix kw- --dry-run                         # Preview
bd rename-prefix kw- --json                            # Apply
```

## Editor Integration

Set up integration with AI coding assistants and IDEs.

```bash
# Setup for different editors/agents
bd setup claude                    # Claude Code - installs hooks
bd setup claude --project          # Project-only installation
bd setup cursor                    # Cursor IDE - creates .cursor/rules/beads.mdc
bd setup aider                     # Aider - creates .aider.conf.yml
bd setup codex                     # Codex CLI - creates AGENTS.md
bd setup factory                   # Factory.ai - creates AGENTS.md

# Check if integration is installed
bd setup claude --check
bd setup cursor --check

# Remove integration
bd setup claude --remove
bd setup cursor --remove

# Install git hooks for automatic sync
bd hooks install
```

## Key-Value Store

Store user-defined key-value pairs that persist across sessions. Useful for feature flags, environment config, or agent memory.

```bash
# Set values
bd kv set feature_flag true
bd kv set api_endpoint https://api.example.com
bd kv set last_migration 20240115_add_users.sql

# Get values
bd kv get feature_flag                 # Output: true
bd kv get missing_key                  # Output: missing_key (not set), exits 1

# Delete keys
bd kv clear feature_flag

# List all key-value pairs
bd kv list --json
# Output: {"feature_flag": "true", "api_endpoint": "https://api.example.com"}
```

## Common Agent Workflow

Complete workflow pattern for AI agents working with beads.

```bash
# 1. Start of session - find available work
bd ready --json

# 2. Claim an issue atomically
bd update bd-42 --claim --json

# 3. Work on the issue...

# 4. Discover related bugs during work
bd create "Found auth bug" -t bug -p 1 --deps discovered-from:bd-42 --json

# 5. Close when done
bd close bd-42 --reason "Implemented and tested" --json

# 6. Check what unblocked
bd blocked

# 7. End of session - force sync
bd sync

# Batch operations
bd update bd-41 bd-42 bd-43 --priority 0 --json        # Update multiple
bd close bd-41 bd-42 bd-43 --reason "Batch done" --json # Close multiple
bd label add bd-41 bd-42 bd-43 urgent --json           # Add labels to multiple
```

Beads is designed for autonomous agent workflows where multiple AI agents work concurrently across branches. The hash-based ID system prevents merge conflicts, while atomic claim operations prevent race conditions. Agents can traverse compound workflows by following the dependency graph, handing off between sessions until all work closes. The molecular chemistry layer enables reusable workflow templates for common patterns like feature development, bug fixes, and patrol cycles.

For human developers, beads provides a command-line interface with both human-readable and JSON output modes. The `bd ready` command surfaces work that can be started immediately, while `bd blocked` shows what's waiting on dependencies. Integration with git hooks enables automatic sync on commit and merge, keeping the issue tracker in sync with code changes. External integrations with Jira, Linear, and GitHub allow bidirectional sync with existing project management tools.
